journal_entry.reason: Target purchased tea, beverages, and refreshments for the employee break room. This is an employee benefit/welfare expense. The $1,200 pre-tax amount is expensed, the $120 sales tax (10%) is recorded as a recoverable input tax credit (asset), and the total $1,320 is charged to the corporate credit card (liability). Under IFRS, sales tax paid on business purchases in Ontario is typically recoverable as HST/GST input tax credit.

output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction records the purchase of employee break room supplies using a corporate credit card. The business purpose is employee welfare, so the $1,200 pre-tax amount is classified as Employee benefits expense. The 10% sales tax ($120) is recoverable input tax in Ontario (HST system), recorded as Tax assets (current asset). The total charge of $1,320 creates a liability to the credit card company. Debits total $1,320 ($1,200 + $120), credits total $1,320, satisfying the double-entry requirement.
